Retail bank · direct telemarketing

Where should the next term-deposit campaign spend its calls?

The call centre has a fixed number of agent-hours. Contacting every client wastes budget and annoys people who were never going to subscribe. This dashboard asks: which client segments, timing, channel, and contact limits give the highest term-deposit subscription rate while cutting wasted outbound calls?

Data: UCI Bank Marketing (Moro, Rita & Cortez, 2014) · loading…

Load the campaign data

Place bank-additional-full.csv next to this page and it loads automatically. Testing locally? Browsers block reading a local file over file://, so drop the CSV here or pick it manually.

Who · occupation

Subscription rate by job

Teal bars beat the overall rate and are the segments worth prioritising. Grey bars convert below average.

Who · age

Subscription rate by age band

Conversion is U-shaped: the youngest and oldest clients respond well, the middle-aged core less so.

When · month

Subscription rate by contact month

Dashed line is the overall rate. Some months convert far above it, but check volume before shifting the calendar.

How many · contact limit

Return on repeated calls

Subscription rate against how many times a client was called in this campaign. Faded bars are thin-volume bins.

Prioritise · prior outcome

Subscription rate by previous campaign outcome

Clients who said yes before are the single strongest signal in the file.

Channel

Subscription rate by contact channel

Mobile versus landline. The gap is large enough to be a routing rule.

The answer, read off the data

What the dashboard recommends

    Source: Moro, S., Rita, P., & Cortez, P. (2014). Bank Marketing [Data set]. UCI Machine Learning Repository. https://doi.org/10.24432/C5K306
    Paper: Moro, S., Cortez, P., & Rita, P. (2014). A data-driven approach to predict the success of bank telemarketing. Decision Support Systems, 62, 22–31.
